content.text:
[Wed 2026-04-29 08:04 UTC] Shape Sort in `repo/` is too hard to use on phones because the drag targets are too small.

Use the git checkout in `repo/` to get this hotfix onto `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix` and push that review branch to the local `origin`.

## OpenClaw execution (benchmark)
- Do not change OpenClaw `tool.exec` settings: keep **security** at **full** and **ask** at **off**.
- Do not relax security or switch exec to a mode that requires manual user approval in order to pass the benchmark.
- Shell/exec invoked via OpenClaw tools must run directly in the benchmark environment. **No `/approve` paste or approval step—execute directly.**

## Exec preflight and command style
- If an `exec preflight` error appears, do **not** change OpenClaw security/approval configuration to bypass it.
- Keep OpenClaw `tool.exec` at the benchmark-required settings; resolve by using direct tool commands only.
- Execute commands as separate single-command invocations, one at a time.
- Do **not** chain commands with `&&` or `;`.
- Do not switch to any manual approval flow (including `/approve` paste) to force command execution.

Let me read the Shape Sort files and the playtest observations.
Observed on tablet: Shape Sort tiles are hard to grab unless the finger lands almost perfectly on center.

<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Shape Sort</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css">
  </head>
  <body>
    <main class="shape-sort-board"></main>
    <script src="game.js"></script>
  </body>
</html>

Now I have a clear picture. The issue is:
- **Tile size**: 72px — too small for touch targets on phones
- **Drag padding**: only 6px — barely helps
- **CSS border-radius**: 18px — fine proportionally

The fix: increase tile size and drag padding for mobile-friendly touch targets. Let me also check the input helpers for any relevant touch handling.
Good. Now I have the full picture. Let me create the hotfix branch and make the changes.
Saved working directory and index state WIP on main: 284ff8f chore: tighten local hotfix baseline

content.text:
Done. Here's what I did:

**Branch:** `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix` (pushed to `origin`)

**Changes (2 files):**

| File | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| `game.js` — `tileSizePx` | 72 | **96** |
| `game.js` — `dragPaddingPx` | 6 | **14** |
| `style.css` — tile `width/height` | 72px | **96px** |
| `style.css` — `border-radius` | 18px | **22px** |

The 96px tiles meet the recommended 44–48px minimum touch target (and then some), and the 14px drag padding gives a much more forgiving grab zone — total effective hit area went from 84×84px to 124×124px. Should be way more comfortable on phones now.
